verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres mei, nolite in personarum acceptione habere fidem Domini nostri Jesu Christi gloriæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀδελφοί μου μὴ ἐν προσωποληψίαις ἔχετε τὴν πίστιν τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τῆς δόξης
verses.king_james: My brethren, have not the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, *the Lord* of glory, with respect of persons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim si introierit in conventum vestrum vir aureum annulum habens in veste candida, introierit autem et pauper in sordido habitu,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν γὰρ εἰσέλθῃ εἰς τήν συναγωγὴν ὑμῶν ἀνὴρ χρυσοδακτύλιος ἐν ἐσθῆτι λαμπρᾷ εἰσέλθῃ δὲ καὶ πτωχὸς ἐν ῥυπαρᾷ ἐσθῆτι
verses.king_james: For if there come unto your assembly a man with a gold ring, in goodly apparel, and there come in also a poor man in vile raiment;

verses.latin_vulgate: et intendatis in eum qui indutus est veste præclara, et dixeritis ei: Tu sede hic bene: pauperi autem dicatis: Tu sta illic; aut sede sub scabello pedum meorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιβλέψητε ἐπὶ τὸν φοροῦντα τὴν ἐσθῆτα τὴν λαμπρὰν καὶ εἴπητε αὐτῷ σὺ κάθου ὧδε καλῶς καὶ τῷ πτωχῷ εἴπητε σὺ στῆθι ἐκεῖ ἢ κάθου ὧδε ὑπὸ τὸ ὑποπόδιόν μου
verses.king_james: And ye have respect to him that weareth the gay clothing, and say unto him, Sit thou here in a good place; and say to the poor, Stand thou there, or sit here under my footstool:

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne judicatis apud vosmetipsos, et facti estis judices cogitationum iniquarum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐ διεκρίθητε ἐν ἑαυτοῖς καὶ ἐγένεσθε κριταὶ διαλογισμῶν πονηρῶν
verses.king_james: Are ye not then partial in yourselves, and are become judges of evil thoughts?

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, fratres mei dilectissimi: nonne Deus elegit pauperes in hoc mundo, divites in fide, et hæredes regni, quod repromisit Deus diligentibus se?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσατε ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί οὐχ ὁ θεὸς ἐξελέξατο τοὺς πτωχοὺς τοῦ κόσμου τούτου πλουσίους ἐν πίστει καὶ κληρονόμους τῆς βασιλείας ἡς ἐπηγγείλατο τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Hearken, my beloved brethren, Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom which he hath promised to them that love him?

verses.latin_vulgate: vos autem exhonorastis pauperem. Nonne divites per potentiam opprimunt vos, et ipsi trahunt vos ad judicia?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ ἠτιμάσατε τὸν πτωχόν οὐχ οἱ πλούσιοι καταδυναστεύουσιν ὑμῶν καὶ αὐτοὶ ἕλκουσιν ὑμᾶς εἰς κριτήρια
verses.king_james: But ye have despised the poor. Do not rich men oppress you, and draw you before the judgment seats?

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne ipsi blasphemant bonum nomen, quod invocatum est super vos?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ αὐτοὶ βλασφημοῦσιν τὸ καλὸν ὄνομα τὸ ἐπικληθὲν ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Do not they blaspheme that worthy name by the which ye are called?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si tamen legem perficitis regalem secundum Scripturas: Diliges proximum tuum sicut teipsum: bene facitis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ μέντοι νόμον τελεῖτε βασιλικὸν κατὰ τὴν γραφήν ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν καλῶς ποιεῖτε
verses.king_james: If ye fulfil the royal law according to the scripture, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself, ye do well:

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem personas accipitis, peccatum operamini, redarguti a lege quasi transgressores.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ προσωποληπτεῖτε ἁμαρτίαν ἐργάζεσθε ἐλεγχόμενοι ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου ὡς παραβάται
verses.king_james: But if ye have respect to persons, ye commit sin, and are convinced of the law as transgressors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quicumque autem totam legem servaverit, offendat autem in uno, factus est omnium reus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅστις γὰρ ὅλον τὸν νόμον τηρήσει πταίσει δὲ ἐν ἑνί γέγονεν πάντων ἔνοχος
verses.king_james: For whosoever shall keep the whole law, and yet offend in one *point*, he is guilty of all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim dixit: Non mœchaberis, dixit et: Non occides. Quod si non mœchaberis, occides autem, factus es transgressor legis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ εἰπών μὴ μοιχεύσῃς εἶπεν καί μὴ φονεύσῃς εἰ δὲ οὐ μοιχεύσεις φονεύσεις δέ γέγονας παραβάτης νόμου
verses.king_james: For he that said, Do not commit adultery, said also, Do not kill. Now if thou commit no adultery, yet if thou kill, thou art become a transgressor of the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic loquimini, et sic facite sicut per legem libertatis incipientes judicari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως λαλεῖτε καὶ οὕτως ποιεῖτε ὡς διὰ νόμου ἐλευθερίας μέλλοντες κρίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: So speak ye, and so do, as they that shall be judged by the law of liberty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judicium enim sine misericordia illi qui non fecit misericordiam: superexaltat autem misericordia judicium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ κρίσις ἀνίλεως τῷ μὴ ποιήσαντι ἔλεος καὶ κατακαυχᾶται ἔλεος κρίσεως
verses.king_james: For he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy; and mercy rejoiceth against judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid proderit, fratres mei, si fidem quis dicat se habere, opera autem non habeat? numquid poterit fides salvare eum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί τὸ ὄφελος ἀδελφοί μου ἐὰν πίστιν λέγῃ τις ἔχειν ἔργα δὲ μὴ ἔχῃ μὴ δύναται ἡ πίστις σῶσαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: What *doth it* profit, my brethren, though a man say he hath faith, and have not works? can faith save him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem frater et soror nudi sint, et indigeant victu quotidiano,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ ἀδελφὸς ἢ ἀδελφὴ γυμνοὶ ὑπάρχωσιν καὶ λειπόμενοι ὦσιν τῆς ἐφημέρου τροφῆς
verses.king_james: If a brother or sister be naked, and destitute of daily food,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicat autem aliquis ex vobis illis: Ite in pace, calefacimini et saturamini: non dederitis autem eis quæ necessaria sunt corpori, quid proderit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴπῃ δέ τις αὐτοῖς ἐξ ὑμῶν ὑπάγετε ἐν εἰρήνῃ θερμαίνεσθε καὶ χορτάζεσθε μὴ δῶτε δὲ αὐτοῖς τὰ ἐπιτήδεια τοῦ σώματος τί τὸ ὄφελος
verses.king_james: And one of you say unto them, Depart in peace, be *ye* warmed and filled; notwithstanding ye give them not those things which are needful to the body; what *doth it* profit?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic et fides, si non habeat opera, mortua est in semetipsa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις ἐὰν μὴ ἔργα ἔχῃ νεκρά ἐστιν καθ᾽ ἑαυτήν
verses.king_james: Even so faith, if it hath not works, is dead, being alone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed dicet quis: Tu fidem habes, et ego opera habeo: ostende mihi fidem tuam sine operibus: et ego ostendam tibi ex operibus fidem meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ ἐρεῖ τις σὺ πίστιν ἔχεις κἀγὼ ἔργα ἔχω δεῖξόν μοι τὴν πίστιν σου ἐκ τῶν ἔργων σου κἀγώ δείξω σοι ἐκ τῶν ἔργων μου τὴν πίστιν μου
verses.king_james: Yea, a man may say, Thou hast faith, and I have works: shew me thy faith without thy works, and I will shew thee my faith by my works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu credis quoniam unus est Deus: bene facis: et dæmones credunt, et contremiscunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ πιστεύεις ὅτι ὁ θεός εἷς ἐστιν καλῶς ποιεῖς καὶ τὰ δαιμόνια πιστεύουσιν καὶ φρίσσουσιν
verses.king_james: Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vis autem scire, o homo inanis, quoniam fides sine operibus mortua est?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θέλεις δὲ γνῶναι ὦ ἄνθρωπε κενέ ὅτι ἡ πίστις χωρὶς τῶν ἔργων νεκρά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: But wilt thou know, O vain man, that faith without works is dead?

verses.latin_vulgate: Abraham pater noster nonne ex operibus justificatus est, offerens Isaac filium suum super altare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀβραὰμ ὁ πατὴρ ἡμῶν οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη ἀνενέγκας ἰσαὰκ τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον
verses.king_james: Was not Abraham our father justified by works, when he had offered Isaac his son upon the altar?

verses.latin_vulgate: Vides quoniam fides cooperabatur operibus illius: et ex operibus fides consummata est?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπεις ὅτι ἡ πίστις συνήργει τοῖς ἔργοις αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἔργων ἡ πίστις ἐτελειώθη
verses.king_james: Seest thou how faith wrought with his works, and by works was faith made perfect?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et suppleta est Scriptura, dicens: Credidit Abraham Deo, et reputatum est illi ad justitiam, et amicus Dei appellatus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπληρώθη ἡ γραφὴ ἡ λέγουσα ἐπίστευσεν δὲ ἀβραὰμ τῷ θεῷ καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην καὶ φίλος θεοῦ ἐκλήθη
verses.king_james: And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham believed God, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness: and he was called the Friend of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videtis quoniam ex operibus justificatur homo, et non ex fide tantum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁρᾶτε τοίνυν ὅτι ἐξ ἔργων δικαιοῦται ἄνθρωπος καὶ οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως μόνον
verses.king_james: Ye see then how that by works a man is justified, and not by faith only.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similiter et Rahab meretrix, nonne ex operibus justificata est, suscipiens nuntios, et alia via ejiciens?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοίως δὲ καὶ ῥαὰβ ἡ πόρνη οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη ὑποδεξαμένη τοὺς ἀγγέλους καὶ ἑτέρᾳ ὁδῷ ἐκβαλοῦσα
verses.king_james: Likewise also was not Rahab the harlot justified by works, when she had received the messengers, and had sent *them* out another way?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim corpus sine spiritu mortuum est, ita et fides sine operibus mortua est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ γὰρ τὸ σῶμα χωρὶς πνεύματος νεκρόν ἐστιν οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις χωρὶς τῶν ἔργων νεκρά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.
